hints: Build the pattern piece by piece: \(? matches an optional opening paren, \d{3} matches exactly three digits, \)? matches an optional closing paren, [-.\ s]? matches an optional separator (hyphen, dot, or space), and so on.
The full pattern is r"\(?\d{3}\)?[-.\s]?\d{3}[-.\s]?\d{4}". Each [-.\ s]? means 'one optional separator character from this set'.
Use no capture groups (no parentheses other than \(? and \)?) so that findall returns a list of full matched strings rather than a list of tuples.
Return PHONE_PATTERN.findall(text) — findall scans the whole string and returns every match as a list. No loop needed.
